digest.en: Trump's Middle East tour dominates the hour, with the US President announcing a cease-fire extension between Israel and Lebanon, while also keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed and threatening Iran with mine-laying vessels. The EU has approved a 90bn euro loan for Ukraine and new sanctions on Russia, a move that contrasts with the US's escalating tensions with Iran. Meanwhile, Iran's leadership is denying reports of a rift within the country's government.
digest.hi: राष्ट्रीय पंचायती राज दिवस आज मनाया जा रहा है। बंगलूरू में फॉक्सकॉन प्लांट के शौचालय में एक भ्रूण मिला है। इस घटना की जांच चल रही है।

articles.title: Trump Says Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Cease-Fire
articles.summary: Trump extended the Israel and Lebanon cease-fire for three more weeks to advance a peace agreement in Iran. The president said the truce in Lebanon would last three more weeks.

articles.title: EU formally approves 90bn euro Ukraine loan and new sanctions on Russia
articles.summary: The EU approved a 90bn euro loan to Ukraine and new Russia sanctions. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy welcomes the development, urging that the first tranche be disbursed by May or June.

articles.title: Trump says he is keeping Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran
articles.summary: Donald Trump said he is keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran. Donald Trump said he is the one keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran. Trump says he is keeping Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran.

articles.title: US boards ship carrying Iran oil as Trump threatens mine-laying vessels
articles.summary: The US intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran. Central Command said it has intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran.

articles.title: Iran dismisses Trump’s claim of leadership rift, says nation is ‘one soul’
articles.summary: Iranian officials denied a leadership rift, citing the nation's "iron unity". Several Iranian officials share same message, emphasising what they call the 'iron unity of the nation and government'. Iran dismisses Trump’s claim of leadership rift, says nation is ‘one soul’.

articles.title: Afghans Who Helped U.S. Forces Say They’re Being Pushed Back to the Taliban
articles.summary: Afghan refugees who helped US forces may be sent to Congo. Once promised a move to the United States, Afghan refugees who helped U. S. forces say they face ‘bad or worse’ options: resettlement to Congo or returning home to live under the Taliban.

articles.title: What is Europe's plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz?
articles.summary: France and the UK are leading a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. France and the UK are leading efforts to establish a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Mysterious Deaths, Disappearances Rattle US, China
articles.summary: At least nine scientists have died under mysterious circumstances, reports from Chinese and overseas media indicate. The cause of these deaths and several disappearances is not specified. Reports from Chinese and overseas media point to at least nine deaths of scientists.

articles.title: Airlines in Europe slash thousands of flights as Iran war cuts jet fuel supplies
articles.summary: European airlines are canceling tens of thousands of flights due to soaring jet fuel costs amid the Iran war. Energy authorities warn of a possible jet fuel shortage if supplies aren't replenished.

articles.title: Trump To Meet Lebanon, Israel Envoys During Peace Talks
articles.summary: Trump will meet Lebanon and Israel envoys during peace talks after a 10-day truce was announced. The United States announced a 10-day truce shortly after the first meeting. Trump To Meet Lebanon, Israel Envoys During Peace Talks.

articles.title: Mojtaba Injured, A 'Board' Of Generals Helps Take The Calls In Iran
articles.summary: Iran's military establishment took control after Mojtaba was injured. The balance of power appears firmly tilted toward the military establishment. Mojtaba Injured, A 'Board' Of Generals Helps Take The Calls In Iran.

articles.title: Air Defence System Activated In Tehran Amid Reports Of Explosions
articles.summary: Tehran activated its air defense system after reports of explosions in several parts of the capital. The Mehr news agency confirmed the activation. Mehr news agency reported that the systems were activated in several parts of the capital.

articles.title: Crash That Killed 2 Americans Is a Reminder of the Depth of Security Ties in Mexico
articles.summary: A crash in northern Mexico killed two CIA officers, sparking questions about authorization and sovereignty. The incident reflects a longstanding security partnership between the US and Mexico. The deaths of two C. I. A.

articles.title: Another US Aircraft Carrier Reaches Gulf, Joins 2 Already Operating There
articles.summary: A US aircraft carrier has joined two already operating in the Gulf, amid rising tensions with Iran. The deployment was reported in a live update on US-Iran war news. US-Iran War News Live Update:.

articles.title: $106 Billion Loan Reflects E.U.’s View That Peace in Ukraine Is Far Away
articles.summary: The E. U. approved a $106 billion loan for Ukraine, heavily weighted toward military spending. This reflects the E. U.'s view that peace in Ukraine is unlikely in the near future. Unlike previous European assistance packages, this one is heavily weighted toward military spending, meant to put Ukraine on solid footing for a long fight.

articles.title: Ceasefire extended: What's next in the Iran war?
articles.summary: The US and Iran have extended their ceasefire with no fixed deadline. Both countries are now seeking ways to protect their interests amid ongoing tensions. With no fixed ceasefire deadline for now, the US and Iran are looking for ways to safeguard their own interests.

articles.title: Before Iran War, China Amassed World's Largest Oil Stockpile
articles.summary: China amassed the world's largest oil stockpile before the Iran war, a move likely aimed at securing its energy supply. China is the world's largest crude oil importer, making this stockpile crucial to its economy.

articles.title: Blasts reported over Tehran, air defences activated
articles.summary: Blasts were reported over Tehran, prompting Iran to activate its air defenses. Iran had previously stated it wouldn't reopen the Strait of Hormuz as long as the US blockades its ports.

articles.title: Russia reports toxic rain, orders locals to stay home after Ukrainian oil strike
articles.summary: Russia reported toxic rain and ordered locals to stay home after a Ukrainian oil strike in Tuapse on April 20. The strike triggered a huge blaze and sent thick black smoke into the sky over the coastal city.

articles.title: Dozens of Mexican mafia members arrested in California crackdown
articles.summary: Forty-three people were indicted on charges including murder and extortion in a California crackdown on the Mexican mafia.

articles.title: Trump tells BBC that King's visit could 'absolutely' help repair relations with UK
articles.summary: US President Trump told the BBC that King Charles' upcoming visit could help repair US-UK relations. He made the comments during a phone interview with the BBC's North America editor.

articles.summary: लुइसियाना के एक मॉल में गोलीबारी हुई, जिसमें एक व्यक्ति की मौत हो गई और पांच लोग घायल हो गए। कई लोगों को गिरफ्तार किया गया है। घटना में दो समूहों के बीच भिड़ंत हुई थी।

articles.title: ट्रंप ने भारत के बारे में 'विवादित' पोस्ट किया शेयर, भारत ने जारी किया बयान
articles.summary: डोनाल्ड ट्रंप ने भारत के बारे में एक विवादित पोस्ट शेयर किया, जिसके बाद भारत ने बयान जारी कर अपनी प्रतिक्रिया दी। भारत में अमेरिकी दूतावास के प्रवक्ता ने कहा कि ट्रंप ने भारत की प्रशंसा की है।

articles.title: क्या रेगिस्तान को हरा भरा बनाया जा सकता है?
articles.summary: रेगिस्तान को हरा-भरा बनाने की संभावना पर विचार किया जा रहा है। आधुनिक तकनीक में ऐसी क्षमता है, लेकिन इसके परिणामों पर विचार करना आवश्यक है। आधुनिक टेक्नोलॉजी में ऐसी क्षमता है कि वो रेगिस्तान को हरा भरा बना सकती है, लेकिन क्या हमें ऐसा करना चाहिए?

articles.title: महिला पर 'बॉन्डेज सेक्स' के तरीके से प्रेमी की हत्या का आरोप, पुलिस ने क्या बताया
articles.summary: एक महिला पर अपने प्रेमी की हत्या का आरोप लगा है, जिसे उसने 'बॉन्डेज सेक्स' के तरीके से मारने का आरोप है। पुलिस ने बताया कि महिला ने अपने प्रेमी को घर बुलाया और फिर उसकी हत्या कर दी।

articles.summary: पाकिस्तानी लोग कमजोर होती ईरानी मुद्रा में निवेश कर रहे हैं। ईरान में जंग शुरू होने के बाद मुद्रा की कीमत गिर गई थी। निवेशकों को इसके जोखिमों का सामना करना पड़ सकता है।

articles.summary: मालेगांव धमाका मामले में अभियुक्तों को डिस्चार्ज कर दिया गया, क्योंकि जांच एजेंसियों ने अलग-अलग कहानियां पेश कीं। अदालत ने कहा कि दोनों एजेंसियों की जांच में विरोधाभास था, जिसके कारण अभियुक्तों को बरी कर दिया गया।
